task: Replace generate_srt.py with improved script-based SRT generator

improvements: Enhanced split_into_caption_blocks: breaks at sentence boundaries AND punctuation
Smarter word accumulation: respects sentence ends (.!?,) and enforces max 12 words per block
Cleaner block timing logic: uses min() to prevent duration overruns
Identical behavior: script IS the caption source, no speech-to-text API
Proportional word-count-based timing across audio duration

test_ep02.action: Regenerated EP02 SRT using improved script

first_segment.text: What if I told you that the reason you are still living

sample_segment_mid.text: paycheck to paycheck has nothing to do with how much money you

changes_from_v65.block_count: 219 → 166 (fewer, larger blocks due to improved punctuation-aware splitting)
changes_from_v65.splitting_logic: Now respects sentence boundaries (.!?,) and enforces max word limit (8 + 4 buffer)
changes_from_v65.block_duration_logic: Cleaner: uses min() instead of explicit if-else for duration ceiling
changes_from_v65.result_quality: Same accurate timing, improved readability with fewer, more natural caption blocks

notes: Improved SRT generator prioritizes sentence breaks and punctuation. 166 blocks vs 219 in v65 = better readability while maintaining accurate proportional timing across 574.75s audio.
